root_text.an4.187:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.an4.187:1.2: Atha kho vassakāro brāhmaṇo magadhamahāmatto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.187:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vassakāro brāhmaṇo magadhamahāmatto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.187:2.1: “Jāneyya nu kho, bho gotama, asappuriso asappurisaṁ:
root_text.an4.187:2.3: “Aṭṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, brāhmaṇa, anavakāso yaṁ asappuriso asappurisaṁ jāneyya:
root_text.an4.187:2.7: “Etampi kho, brāhmaṇa, aṭṭhānaṁ anavakāso yaṁ asappuriso sappurisaṁ jāneyya:
root_text.an4.187:2.9: “Jāneyya nu kho, bho gotama, sappuriso sappurisaṁ:
root_text.an4.187:2.11: “Ṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, brāhmaṇa, vijjati yaṁ sappuriso sappurisaṁ jāneyya:
root_text.an4.187:2.15: “Etampi kho, brāhmaṇa, ṭhānaṁ vijjati yaṁ sappuriso asappurisaṁ jāneyya:
root_text.an4.187:3.3: ‘aṭṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, brāhmaṇa, anavakāso yaṁ asappuriso asappurisaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an4.187:3.5: Etampi kho, brāhmaṇa, aṭṭhānaṁ anavakāso yaṁ asappuriso sappurisaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an4.187:3.7: Ṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, brāhmaṇa, vijjati yaṁ sappuriso sappurisaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an4.187:3.9: Etampi kho, brāhmaṇa, ṭhānaṁ vijjati yaṁ sappuriso asappurisaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an4.187:4.1: Ekamidaṁ, bho gotama, samayaṁ todeyyassa brāhmaṇassa parisati parūpārambhaṁ vattenti:
root_text.an4.187:4.2: ‘bālo ayaṁ rājā eḷeyyo samaṇe rāmaputte abhippasanno, samaṇe ca pana rāmaputte evarūpaṁ paramanipaccakāraṁ karoti, yadidaṁ abhivādanaṁ paccuṭṭhānaṁ añjalikammaṁ sāmīcikammanti.
root_text.an4.187:4.4: yamako moggallo uggo nāvindakī gandhabbo aggivesso, ye samaṇe rāmaputte abhippasannā, samaṇe ca pana rāmaputte evarūpaṁ paramanipaccakāraṁ karonti, yadidaṁ abhivādanaṁ paccuṭṭhānaṁ añjalikammaṁ sāmīcikamman’ti.
root_text.an4.187:4.5: Tyāssudaṁ todeyyo brāhmaṇo iminā nayena neti.
root_text.an4.187:4.7: paṇḍito rājā eḷeyyo karaṇīyādhikaraṇīyesu vacanīyādhivacanīyesu alamatthadasatarehi alamatthadasataro’ti?
root_text.an4.187:4.8: ‘Evaṁ, bho, paṇḍito rājā eḷeyyo karaṇīyādhikaraṇīyesu vacanīyādhivacanīyesu alamatthadasatarehi alamatthadasataroti.
root_text.an4.187:5.1: Yasmā ca kho, bho, samaṇo rāmaputto raññā eḷeyyena paṇḍitena paṇḍitataro karaṇīyādhikaraṇīyesu vacanīyādhivacanīyesu alamatthadasatarena alamatthadasataro, tasmā rājā eḷeyyo samaṇe rāmaputte abhippasanno, samaṇe ca pana rāmaputte evarūpaṁ paramanipaccakāraṁ karoti, yadidaṁ abhivādanaṁ paccuṭṭhānaṁ añjalikammaṁ sāmīcikammaṁ’.
root_text.an4.187:6.3: yamako moggallo uggo nāvindakī gandhabbo aggivesso, karaṇīyādhikaraṇīyesu vacanīyādhivacanīyesu alamatthadasatarehi alamatthadasatarā’ti?
root_text.an4.187:6.5: yamako moggallo uggo nāvindakī gandhabbo aggivesso, karaṇīyādhikaraṇīyesu vacanīyādhivacanīyesu alamatthadasatarehi alamatthadasatarāti.
root_text.an4.187:7.1: Yasmā ca kho, bho, samaṇo rāmaputto rañño eḷeyyassa parihārakehi paṇḍitehi paṇḍitataro karaṇīyādhikaraṇīyesu vacanīyādhivacanīyesu alamatthadasatarehi alamatthadasataro, tasmā rañño eḷeyyassa parihārakā samaṇe rāmaputte abhippasannā;
root_text.an4.187:7.2: samaṇe ca pana rāmaputte evarūpaṁ paramanipaccakāraṁ karonti, yadidaṁ abhivādanaṁ paccuṭṭhānaṁ añjalikammaṁ sāmīcikamman’ti.
root_text.an4.187:8.1: Acchariyaṁ, bho gotama, abbhutaṁ, bho gotama.
root_text.an4.187:8.3: ‘aṭṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, brāhmaṇa, anavakāso yaṁ asappuriso asappurisaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an4.187:8.5: Etampi kho, brāhmaṇa, aṭṭhānaṁ anavakāso yaṁ asappuriso sappurisaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an4.187:8.7: Ṭhānaṁ kho etaṁ, brāhmaṇa, vijjati yaṁ sappuriso sappurisaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an4.187:8.9: Etampi kho, brāhmaṇa, ṭhānaṁ vijjati yaṁ sappuriso asappurisaṁ jāneyya—
root_text.an4.187:8.11: Handa ca dāni mayaṁ, bho gotama, gacchāma.
root_text.an4.187:9.1: Atha kho vassakāro brāhmaṇo magadhamahāmatto bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmīti.

translation_text.an4.187:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.an4.187:1.2: Then Vassakāra the brahmin, a chief minister of Magadha, went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.187:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an4.187:2.1: “Worthy Gotama, could an untrue person know of an untrue person:
translation_text.an4.187:2.2: ‘This worthy one is an untrue person’?”
translation_text.an4.187:2.5: “Could an untrue person know of a true person:
translation_text.an4.187:2.6: ‘This worthy one is a true person’?”
translation_text.an4.187:2.7: “That too is impossible, it can’t happen.”
translation_text.an4.187:2.9: “Worthy Gotama, could a true person know of a true person:
translation_text.an4.187:2.10: ‘This worthy one is a true person’?”
translation_text.an4.187:2.13: “Could a true person know of an untrue person:
translation_text.an4.187:2.14: ‘This worthy one is an untrue person’?”
translation_text.an4.187:3.2: how well said this was by the worthy Gotama:
translation_text.an4.187:3.3: ‘It’s impossible, it can’t happen, that an untrue person could know …
translation_text.an4.187:3.7: But it is possible that a true person could know …’
translation_text.an4.187:4.1: Once, members of the brahmin Todeyya’s assembly were going on complaining about others:
translation_text.an4.187:4.2: ‘This King Eḷeyya is a fool to be so devoted to the ascetic Rāmaputta. He even shows him the utmost deference by bowing down to him, rising up for him, greeting him with joined palms, and observing proper etiquette for him.
translation_text.an4.187:4.3: And these king’s men are fools too—
translation_text.an4.187:4.4: Yamaka, Moggalla, Ugga, Nāvindakī, Gandhabba, and Aggivessa—for they show the same kind of deference to Rāmaputta.’
translation_text.an4.187:4.5: Then the brahmin Todeyya reasoned with them like this:
translation_text.an4.187:4.7: When it comes to the various duties and speeches, isn’t King Eḷeyya astute, even better than the experts?’
translation_text.an4.187:5.1: ‘It’s because the ascetic Rāmaputta is even more astute and expert than King Eḷeyya that the king is so devoted to him. That’s why he even shows Rāmaputta the utmost deference by bowing down to him, rising up for him, greeting him with joined palms, and observing proper etiquette for him.
translation_text.an4.187:6.3: When it comes to the various duties and speeches, aren’t the king’s men—Yamaka, Moggalla, Ugga, Nāvindakī, Gandhabba, and Aggivessa—astute, even better than the experts?’
translation_text.an4.187:7.1: ‘It’s because Rāmaputta is even more astute and expert than the king’s men that they have such devotion to him. …
translation_text.an4.187:7.2: It’s because Rāmaputta is even more astute and expert than King Eḷeyya that the king is so devoted to him. That’s why he even shows Rāmaputta the utmost deference by bowing down to him, rising up for him, greeting him with joined palms, and observing proper etiquette for him.’
translation_text.an4.187:8.1: It’s incredible, worthy Gotama, it’s amazing,
translation_text.an4.187:8.2: how well said this was by the worthy Gotama:
translation_text.an4.187:8.3: ‘It’s impossible, it can’t happen, that an untrue person could know …
translation_text.an4.187:8.7: But it is possible that a true person could know …
translation_text.an4.187:8.11: Well, now, worthy Gotama, I must go.
translation_text.an4.187:8.12: I have many duties, and much to do.”
translation_text.an4.187:9.1: Then Vassakāra the brahmin, having approved and agreed with what the Buddha said, got up from his seat and left.
